skill.name: context-window-management
skill.description: Guidance for managing LLM context windows with summarization, trimming, routing, token awareness, and priority placement.
skill.summary: Manage long LLM conversations by curating context before token limits reduce answer quality.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged inline backtick text and a weak-crypto pattern, but both were false positives in Markdown prose. No command execution, cryptographic implementation, network access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False positive: inline related skill names
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated Markdown backticks around related skill names as Ruby or shell execution. The line only lists companion skills and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected text is Markdown inline code inside a related-skills sentence. There is no shell syntax, script block, or user input execution path.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography keyword
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged the description as a weak cryptographic algorithm. The phrase describes context quality degradation and contains no cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is front matter prose describing context management. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or security-sensitive algorithm appears there.

content.value_statement: Long conversations can lose important details or exceed token limits. This skill guides summarization, trimming, routing, and prioritization for reliable LLM workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to summarize, trim, retrieve, or route context.
Prioritizes important information for long conversations.
Applies serial position guidance to place key details effectively.
Identifies anti-patterns such as naive truncation and uniform context handling.
Helps plan token-aware workflows for LLM applications.
Provides a vocabulary for context engineering decisions.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts or automated token counters.
Does not connect to external retrieval systems or databases.
Provides strategy guidance rather than benchmarked performance guarantees.
Requires the user to supply the conversation or application context.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Support Conversations
use_cases.description: Design a summary and retention plan for long customer support threads that must preserve unresolved issues.

use_cases.title: Improve Coding Sessions
use_cases.description: Keep requirements, decisions, and current files visible during extended Claude Code or Codex work.

use_cases.title: Plan Context Routing
use_cases.description: Choose which documents, memories, or summaries should enter each model call for a product workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Context Risks
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a long prompt or conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this conversation for context window risks. Identify information that should be kept, summarized, trimmed, or moved to retrieval.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Summary Policy
prompt_templates.scenario: Define a repeatable approach for long sessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a context summary policy for this workflow. Include what to preserve, when to summarize, and what can be discarded.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Routing Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Select context for different task types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context routing strategy for these task categories. Explain which context each category needs and why.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Context Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced evaluation of an existing LLM application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this LLM context architecture for token efficiency, lost-in-the-middle risk, summary quality, retrieval fit, and failure modes.

output_examples.input: A 60-message troubleshooting thread keeps losing the original user requirement.
output: Keep the original requirement near the start of each working context.
Summarize resolved diagnostic steps after each major change.
Preserve current blockers, accepted decisions, and next actions near the end.

output_examples.input: A product assistant needs different context for onboarding, billing, and technical support questions.
output: Route policy and account context to billing requests.
Route setup goals and user profile context to onboarding requests.
Route logs, recent errors, and product documentation to technical support requests.

best_practices: Place critical goals, constraints, and current decisions where the model can easily use them.
Summarize by importance and task relevance, not only by message age.
Separate stable reference material from short-lived working context.
anti_patterns: Do not remove old messages only because they are old.
Do not send every available document when a narrow task needs only a few sources.
Do not rely on one context strategy for every workflow stage.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a guidance-only skill written in Markdown.

faq.question: Can it count tokens automatically?
faq.answer: No. It explains token-aware strategy, but it does not include a token counting tool.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to long interactions in all three tools.

faq.question: What problem does it solve?
faq.answer: It helps prevent important information from being lost when prompts or conversations become long.

faq.question: Does it replace retrieval augmented generation?
faq.answer: No. It helps decide when retrieval is better than adding more text to the prompt.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Use it when designing assistants, long coding sessions, support workflows, or any token-limited LLM process.
